title: What's the real return?
intro: A headline return is before inflation. Estimate what actually grows your purchasing power.
prompt: A fund returns 12% nominal while inflation runs at 6%. Estimate the approximate real (inflation-adjusted) return.
debrief: Real return is (1.12 / 1.06 − 1) ≈ 5.7%, not the tempting 12% − 6% = 6%. At higher inflation the subtraction overstates your real gain even more. Always convert headline returns to real before deciding they're 'enough.'
